selected_important_turn.text: So in order to fully enjoy my on this trip while surviving, I will require mass quantities of food.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This early strong demand sets a zero-sum, competitive tone and anchors the negotiation around you taking 'mass quantities' of the highest-value item. It shapes how the opponent responds and reduces openness to tradeoffs.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The line is uncompromising and vague about tradeable terms — it asserts entitlement rather than inviting information or integrative exchange. Because it appears early, a softer/strategic alternative could have prevented escalation and opened room for value-creating trades.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Soften the claim and convert it into an information- or trade-seeking move: state that food is your top priority but ask the opponent about their needs and offer to explore exchanges (e.g., "I value food most — what would you trade for me getting more of it?"). Avoid absolute, maximal language and anchor toward problem-solving.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: likely increase (opens integrative possibilities instead of hardening claims)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (reduces heavy anchoring that biases allocations)

value2reason.Low: firewood easy to get all are bring firewood
value2reason.High: The first priority was food food very essential

value2reason.Low: As long as I have a minimum amount of water to get by day to day, I will be fine.
value2reason.Medium: In order to cook the amount of food I require to survive and have a good time, I need extra firewood.
value2reason.High: To have a good time, I need energy camping.  For energy I require food.

dialogue.text: So in order to fully enjoy my on this trip while surviving, I will require mass quantities of food.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I value food most — what would you trade for me getting more of it?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I agree food is key. Let's split the 3 Food packages evenly—1 each. For Water, I’ll give you 2 and keep 1. Firewood, I’ll take all 3 since it’s low priority for me. That gives me 5+4+9 = 18 points. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I appreciate the offer, but I need more food. How about I take 2 Food, 1 Firewood, and 1 Water? That gives me 10 + 4 + 3 = 17 points. Let me know if you're open to adjusting the split.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I understand your need for food. Let's adjust—give you 2 Food, 1 Water, and 1 Firewood. I'll take 1 Food, 2 Water, and 2 Firewood. That gives me 5 + 8 + 6 = 19 points. It balances our priorities. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Your offer balances our needs well. I'll accept 2 Food, 1 Water, and 1 Firewood. That gives me 10 + 3 + 4 = 17 points. Let's finalize this split.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Deal confirmed! You get 2 Food, 1 Water, and 1 Firewood. I take 1 Food, 2 Water, and 2 Firewood. All packages are split. Let's head out!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
